mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or event-study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals by > 5% annualized, or 60-day forward returns show negative mean during concurrent expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost inflation pressures and supports equity valuations over the next month during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of rising INDPRO observations when FEDFUNDS is flat, or rolling correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels above 252.0 indicate persistent inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 252.0, or event-study returns are statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue generation, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings growth.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of consecutive decline instances, or event-study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
